title: Myogenic Response
buzzwords: Intrinsic autoregulation independent of nerves and hormones
Rises in pressure trigger reflex vessel constriction
Engages within seconds of pressure change
Vessel wall tension drives the feedback
Buffers transient swings from posture or activity
Operates across MAP roughly 80 to 180
Stretch opens calcium channels in smooth muscle
Afferent arteriole constricts when wall is stretched

title: Tubuloglomerular Feedback
buzzwords: Tubular signal feeds back onto upstream arteriole
Engages over 10 to 15 seconds
Matches filtered load to reabsorptive capacity
High tubular flow shortens reabsorption time
Specialized thick ascending limb cells sense salt
High distal salt triggers afferent constriction
Macula densa senses tubular NaCl concentration
Adenosine and ATP release constrict afferent arteriole

title: Sympathetic Regulation of Renal Vasculature
buzzwords: Extrinsic neural control overrides autoregulation during stress
Dominant in hemorrhage and hypovolemic shock
Norepinephrine from dense postganglionic innervation
Can reduce renal blood flow by 30 to 50 percent
Increases tubular sodium reabsorption alongside constriction
Also triggers renin release from juxtaglomerular cells
Alpha-1 receptors on afferent and efferent arterioles
Predominantly afferent constriction lowering both RBF and GFR

title: Angiotensin II (RAAS) and Renal Hemodynamics
buzzwords: Circulating hormonal vasoconstrictor of renal arterioles
Activated by hypotension and low distal sodium
Preserves filtration despite falling renal blood flow
Raises glomerular hydrostatic pressure selectively
Maintains GFR while RBF drops, raising filtration fraction
Acts through AT1 receptors on arteriolar smooth muscle
Renin from juxtaglomerular cells initiates the cascade
Preferential efferent arteriolar constriction

title: Renal Prostaglandins
buzzwords: Locally produced vasodilator paracrine mediator
Especially important when perfusion is low
Counterbalances sympathetic and angiotensin II constriction
Protects GFR in heart failure, cirrhosis, volume depletion
Cyclooxygenase-derived arachidonic acid products
PGE2 and PGI2 dilate afferent arteriole
Inhibition by NSAIDs precipitates acute kidney injury
Afferent dilation preserves flow during hypoperfusion
